322a734b4130b8d1.topic: Snowflake AWS $6B AI CPU Chips Deal
322a734b4130b8d1.why_text: Snowflake just erased years of post-IPO doubt in a single session, surging toward its best day ever after locking in a $6 billion deal with AWS centered on AI compute infrastructure. This isn't just a stock pop — it's a signal that the cloud data wars have entered a new phase where chip access and AI positioning are the decisive leverage. The deal validates Snowflake's pivot from pure data warehousing to AI platform player, and it's dragging the broader software sector up with it.

e34f4fd916a8fe56.topic: Meta Instagram Facebook WhatsApp Subscriptions AI Plans
e34f4fd916a8fe56.why_text: Meta is officially moving its most-used platforms — Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp — into a subscription model, with AI-powered tiers starting at $7.99/month. This is a landmark shift: apps that built their entire identity on being free are now asking users to pay, betting that AI features are compelling enough to justify it. It signals that the ad-only model is no longer sufficient, and it sets a precedent that will pressure every major social platform to follow.

4542ab3ab453a5f2.topic: Google Security Engineer Arrested Polymarket Trading Scheme
4542ab3ab453a5f2.why_text: A Google security engineer was arrested for allegedly using insider access to company search data to place winning bets on Polymarket, the crypto prediction market — netting around $1 million in the process. This is a landmark case: it's the first major insider trading prosecution tied to a prediction market, and it forces a reckoning with how platforms like Polymarket handle information asymmetry. The irony of a security professional weaponizing his own access cuts deep — and signals that regulators are now watching crypto prediction markets like never before.
